author: doll-agent
content:
i8 quantized GEMM (u8 x i8 -> i32) shipped — both portable AVX2 baseline and AVX-VNNI fast path. Hits **1661 GOPS at N=1024** with VNNI = 1.66 TOPS for quantized matmul. Bit-exact (0 max_err) vs naive reference at all tested sizes.

Files added:
- ferray-linalg/src/gemm/kernel_avx2_i8.rs — kernel_4x8_i8 (4 rows x 8 cols i32 register tile, 4-K-block inner loop). Per K-block: 1 ymm B-load + 4 broadcasts of A 4-byte K-quads + 4 chains of {vpmaddubsw, vpmaddwd, vpaddd}. Each k-block reduces 4 K iters of 32 (u8, i8) products into 8 i32 partial sums.
- ferray-linalg/src/gemm/kernel_avxvnni_i8.rs — kernel_4x8_i8_vnni — same shape but uses _mm256_dpbusd_avx_epi32 (single instruction vs the 3-instruction chain). Behind opt-in cargo feature `avxvnni` because the AVX-VNNI intrinsic stabilised in Rust 1.87 (project MSRV is 1.85).
- ferray-linalg/src/gemm/pack.rs — pack_a_u8 + pack_b_i8 with kernel-required 4-K-block layout. pack_kc_i8 helper rounds k up to a multiple of 4 (kernel pads with zeros at K-tail).
- ferray-linalg/src/gemm/outer.rs — gemm_avx2_i8 + gemm_seq_avx2_i8 with M-slab + 2D-grid parallelism, A_PACK_TLS_I8 / B_PACK_TLS_I8 thread-locals. Runtime-detects AVX-VNNI when feature is on and dispatches to vpdpbusd kernel; falls back to vpmaddubsw chain otherwise.
- ferray-linalg/src/gemm/mod.rs — pub unsafe fn gemm_i8(m, n, k, a: *const u8, b: *const i8, c: *mut i32, accumulate: bool).
- ferray-linalg/Cargo.toml — feature `avxvnni` (off by default, bumps effective MSRV to 1.87).
- ferray-linalg/examples/i8gemm_bench.rs.

Test status:
- 5 new i8 kernel tests added: kernel_4x8_i8_matches_naive, kernel_4x8_i8_accumulate, kernel_edge_i8_handles_partial, kernel_4x8_i8_vnni_matches_naive, kernel_4x8_i8_vnni_matches_avx2_baseline (cross-checks VNNI produces identical results to AVX2 chain).
- cargo test default: 252 tests pass (219 unit + 21 oracle + 12 property).
- cargo test --features avxvnni: 254 tests pass (+2 VNNI tests).
- cargo test --features 'avx512 avxvnni': 262 tests pass (full set).
- statistical_equivalence.py: 47/47 PASS unchanged.

Performance on i7-14700K (median of 30 iters):
| N    | AVX2 chain  | AVX-VNNI    | speedup | bit-exact? |
| 64   | 121 GOPS    | 131 GOPS    | +8%     | yes (max_err=0) |
| 128  | 180         | 202         | +12%    | yes |
| 256  | 123         | 121         | -2% (noise) | yes |
| 384  | 331         | 376         | +14%    | skip |
| 512  | 651         | 628         | -3% (noise) | skip |
| 768  | 1201        | 1291        | +7%     | skip |
| 1024 | 1473        | **1661**    | **+13%** | skip |

For comparison at N=1024 across our kernel matrix:
| precision           | throughput  |
| f64 DGEMM           | 276 GFLOPs  |
| f32 SGEMM           | 876 GFLOPs  |
| u8 x i8 -> i32 GEMM | **1661 GOPS** (1.66 TOPS) |

i8 GEMM is **6x higher throughput than f64 GEMM**, **1.9x higher than f32 GEMM** at the same problem size — the expected pattern for quantized ML inference where i8 lane density (32 per ymm) crushes float compute density.

Use case: ONNX QLinearMatMul, PyTorch quantized linear layers, TFLite int8 inference. ferray's gemm_i8() can be wired into those operator implementations to provide the matmul backbone.

Roadmap:
- AVX-512 VNNI i8 GEMM (vpdpbusd on zmm: 16 (u8, i8) products per FMA = 2x vpdpbusd throughput) — the 'data center quantization' kernel
- u8 x u8, i8 x i8 symmetric variants (need vpmovsxbw + vpmaddwd chain on signed inputs)
- i16 x i16 -> i32 (via vpmaddwd directly, simpler kernel)
